author: @realherbalism1017
text: Just subscribed & you have an awesome channel. You may not realize how close to the actual truth you really are when you call technological marvels magic. Modern people tend to think of magic as spells & rituals & this was also the case with more ancient peoples but there was a difference. To the ancient peoples, anything that worked but some unknown means was magic. Giambattista della Porta's 1584 work "Natural Magick" proves this point admirably. The whole book is filled with a variety of experiments that actually work & now have sound reasons for their working. Then however, the why of how these things worked was a mystery. This definition actually stretches back at least a couple of thousand years.

author: @selfcorrected-Bobby
text: I still believe Earnshaw's theorem creates an opportunity for a maglev bobbin whose topology will create a frictionally non-mechanical interface. The problem will be temperatures and an absence of atmospheric resistance however I think the absurdities of superconductive material experiments have led to a huge disarray of misguided attempts while not addressing the real problem even on a crude level. Sorry to ramble - I know it's possible I just can't prove it yet, I just do lowly webdesign stuff but again I think tetrateanite and maglev dynamos will be a huge boon oneday. It seems that the levitational bearing and its spin are confounded with the other aspet of the  alternating magnetic array so that people seem to disregar that magnetic topology can compensate for Earnshaw's theorem and that a stable spin is possible without a centra mechanical bearing.I will do this with styrafoam and tiny magnets when I can afford them some day as it seems people don't get my point and think I am bereft of reason which I know I am not. Beam me up (too), Scotty!.

replies.author: @selfcorrected-Bobby
replies.text: It is not somuch a bobbin as it repels threads of energy - it is much like a magnetic field "'acquiescer'' that translates alternating current unto a recepticle. This ""interlocutory" imposition is like an infinitely dodgy politician that piggy-backs differing opinions while balancing a ''dynamically perpetuated discord'' of stability requirements that is considerate of Earnshaw's theorem.

author: @Cryptic_Triptych
text:
Magic, you say? It seems to me that word, like most these days, has lost its meaning. Magic is not some parlor trick, slight of hand or waving some wand. The art of Spelling, or casting a spell, incantations, witchcraft, being Wiccan or even War Ship, have also all lost their meaning. There have been some crafty people who have changed the meanings of words by use of sword, even replacing, i am ai. The goal is to invert and pervert, to attract and subvert. Propagandize and Evangelize, hidden so no one will realize. They serve, the demiurge. Criticize and monetize. Real men, they demoralize. Production lines of crazy Karen eyes. More predictions of drug addictions, while they burn the files of Wall Street pedophiles.

Magic is the ability to manifest from the noumenal Realm into the phenomenal Realm. There is no effort required, it is as easy as relaxing and being in the "Zone". You want to make magic, stand up and fight the good fight, every moment, every minute, every day. Feel the power coursing through your viens. Feel how a Warlock, his strength, he gains.

replies.author: @ThinkingandTinkering
replies.text: nothing has meaning without context mate - even what you just said

replies.author: @Cryptic_Triptych
replies.text: @ThinkingandTinkering  words have meaning, by definition. When authorities change definitions of words to make them less meaningful or altogether meaningless, then people lose power. Words are weapons, sharper than knives. Words are swords. Words have power, cursing, cursive, spell, spelling. Words are also frequency. How you speak or tone of frequency and emphasis can be memorizing, entracing. This is why what you say and how you say it is so important. If you understand the cymatics of words, I'm sure you will understand what I'm trying to convey.

replies.author: @ThinkingandTinkering
replies.text: ​@Cryptic_Triptych Oh i get it mate - i just don't agree with you - When I read what you write what i read is someone trying to be deep - what I think you need is clarity in what you say. I mean this most helpfully i really do - but try to make you point as clearly as you can in a way your listener will grasp - What happens when you communicate is you think, put it into words and speak or write - i Hear it or read it, translate it and put it into my thoughts - if that fails you have failed to communicate.

replies.author: @Cryptic_Triptych
replies.text: @ThinkingandTinkering  fair enough. I've spent quite a lot of years examining Essoterics, Occult, the Amun Ra, and I am Gnostic. But, you are definitely entitled to your opinion.

replies.author: @ThinkingandTinkering
replies.text: @Cryptic_Triptych and you to yours mate - cheers

author: @Ferd414
text: Robert - Whilst using some of your advice to construct a serpentine coil for a homebrewed generator, and having a natter with ChatGPT on the math involved in Faraday's law that underlies it, I've come up with an idea I'd like to bounce off you as the "most expert I know of" person on the topic as a sort of "sanity check" before I actually try it, and in the process, destroy umpty-nine miles of expensive fine-drawn enameled copper wire. It seems so obvious (to me, anyway) that I wonder if you (or someone) haven't tried it yourself, and perhaps found it "ungood" for some reason, thus explaining why you aren't using it (particularly since you seem like such a fan of serpentine coils in their various forms and applications) The idea is a bit too complex to try to handle via pure text in youtube comments, though... Some sketches clarify it and save LOTS of yapping that might (as happened in my conversation with chatGPT) be misunderstood/misconstrued and lead to the premature conclusion "That'll never work" (or almost as bad - "SURE! That ought to work even better!" then finding out that I've mutilated quite a few bucks worth of good copper wire to no purpose  - You know what they say - smart is learning from your own mistakes, wise is learning from SOMEBODY ELSE'S mistakes :) ) You got a preferred way to handle such discussions, and a willingness to enter into one?

author: @disklamer
text:
I-magi-nation is our actual magic, it is our innate ability to combine ideas into new concepts, it is the same word but understood differently.
A mage is the one who is able to do all things physical, mental and ephemeral.
Engineers often refer to sorcery being the ingredient that allows disparate elements to combine into greater value.
Clarke's "Any sufficiently advanced technology is indistinguishable from magic" applies wholeheartedly.

@ThinkingandTinkering  do, or do not do, there is no try "YODA"   i can agree to disagree 😉  i find all the time i will think of an idea and next thing you know someone else is always creating or re creating my ideas. I am also very good at manifesting my reality. If i want something bad enough, i do not use my  resources to acquire it. It will either come to me for free, or i will create it for myself.  Everything comes from thoughts multiplied by emotion to manifest reality true.  

Secret to life very few are aware of. Until you pay attention to it. We are independent as well as we are connected as one. Experiencing ourselves as self through everything else.  The definition of magic is just as broad as the definition of what God is. 
Perception of all meaning is infinite it just depends where you stand in the grand scheme of all things.

There is much more to existence ✨️ especially when we have individual experiences that can't be explained by science or faith. 

We also have to realize  we Experience and create everything from words to definitions all ideology is man made reflection of evolved understanding and this in itself can be blinding to see what no one is looking for.  Because we do not know why or how to exist as an individual or collective because raised to believe in what success truly is?

When i say all thoughts become Things i am even including those who do not put the work in, they complain about job before attempting it, they dread life, make exuses these are also things that you could call dark magic 🎩😃

author: @ArcSolaire
text:
When I was younger, I used to wish I was magic every time a wishing opportunity popped up (birthdays, shooting stars etc.) and every time I would play a D&D game I would always think sorcerers were so much cooler than wizards because sorcs just innately had magic and wizards had to study for their entire lives and work very hard.

But as I matured and studied engineering, I came to the same realization that you say here, that technology is magic and my respect for the wizard class grew. It hit me pretty hard that yes, we can be wizards in real life. We just have to put in the effort.

Love you man, thanks for the years of effort you've put in to help people like me! It doesn't go unappreciated.

author: @TheWascallyWelder.1
text: I won't be one to mislead myself in any way, shape, or form about magic. It's not magic to turn on a light switch it's the force of my hand turning on the switch and it's that same force that turns on the light bulb because I let a force called electrical magnetism to energize some copper wire running through my house. Magnetism, as you know, is a force of aligned atoms that creates a magnetic field,  just like the earth has a magnetic field as it's being pulled around the sun while it's in orbit. You talked about people being super hero's  but let me ask you something who makes people "super hero's " is it your amagination or is it their imagination that makes them super hero's. If you didn't imagine that somebody was a superhero then they wouldn't be a superhero they would just be a person to you. And heroes don't exist unless you imagine that Heroes exist that is the facts of it. I for one don't look at his people's Heroes because I'm not going to idolize those people like they deserve some type of worship from me. Are you somebody who wants to worship another imperfect human being who will pass away?

author: @deltonlomatai2309
text: The court magicians/priest in antiquity relied on observation, engineering, math and drugs to do their wonders. They also laid the ground work for classical learning.  Greek temples in this regard where master pieces of engineering to give the effect of being in the presences of deity.  Herons of Alexandria was a master in building devices that beguiled the world.  In the new world and among the soothsayers of antiquity amassed a library of drugs that was amazing.  Ironically it was the knowledge that this was done that caused the intellectuals of the enlightenment to reject miracles and magic as being mystical.

author: @Johnson_Rice
text: There is a disconnect for me on this topic. I don't undertstand even the desire to appeal to people who like to go on about magic in the more traditional sense. I'm perfectly OK distinguishing between magic and science. Most people I've encountered in sciences are in fact using the scientific method and therefore are inherently interested in finding truth. Most poeople that I have encountered in spheres of 'magic' are interested in trickery and illusion and are inherently interested in lying and lies. I do legitimately think I may have some kind of shortcoming in that I don't see any value at all in trying to modify language to appeal to liars... be they talking about free energy with magnets, ghost detectors, dieties, or those that lie about obervation vs instrumentaion affecting quantum experiments... Whatever kinds of lies it may be. I guess maybe I am more of a James Randi-an in that way. Yet, I still feel like I am somehow missing something.

replies.author: @ThinkingandTinkering
replies.text: I never like to say these things mate because I don't like to cast an shadow on someone's thoughts and sometimes it can seem like that even if it is not meant. But, I appreciate the honesty of your comment. But, I believe you are missing something - science isn't a search for truth it is a belief system - It takes it's fundamental belief from the idea that the world is real and therefore measurable and quantifiable - that is by no means certain. Don't get me wrong - I am a scientist through and through - but I also recognize that i believe in science and that i live in a real world. However Bertrand Russel argued quite convincingly that relativity proved that matter didn't really exist - you should read his book - after all Einstein himself turned to God in his later life.

replies.author: @Johnson_Rice
replies.text:
@ 

I just finished with “The Singularity is Nearer” so my queue has an open slot right now - thank you. . Bertrand Russell has a lot on similar topics but I used chatGPT to figure out (I think) that it’s “An Analysis of Matter” that you are talking about and it looks fascinating. 

I personally define reality and truth very similarly. I don’t disagree with the overall statement that science is a belief system, however I still hold to the idea that the scientific method is a system for finding truth or at the least revealing falsities. (To be fair it is certainly far more of the latter) 

My issue is that I came to atheism at a very young age age to see that humans are creatures very very prone to pattern recognition errors, and most are not content with a constant search for answers - preferring to opt to take shortcuts and call it done.  

I am as content with “I don’t know” as I am discontent with those who claim to know what is unknowable. 

It’s one of the things about your approach the this channel that I find delightful.

author: @DeclanMBrennan
text:
Murray Smith's Laws of Magic:
*First Law*
Everything continues in a state of mundaneness, unless acted upon by enthusiasm.
*Second Law*
When effort is exerted to move a concept from vague idea to practical application, magic is said to be done.
*Third Law*
For every setback, there is an equal and opposite revelation.

replies.text: @realorfake4765 Great question! ;-)

author: @lv_woodturner3899
text:
This makes me think of the Arthur C. Clarke quote which was something like "Any sufficiently advanced technology is indistinguishable from magic".
I often wonder how things which we take for granted today would appear to be magic hundreds of years ago.   Simple battery and light bulb for instance.

replies.author: @realorfake4765
replies.text: Flip on the light switch. To the 17th century citizen it's magic.

replies.author: @ChiefBridgeFuser
replies.text: "Automagically" becomes a useful word, knowing this!

replies.author: @ThinkingandTinkering
replies.text: absolutely

replies.author: @vexy1987
replies.text: Rediscover the magic with this thought exercise. You can recall that feeling of wonder, remember batteries are really not that 'simple'. Don't believe me? Try build one yourself from scratch! And hey presto, they are magic again.

replies.author: @j.christie2594
replies.text:
I built Tesla coil.
Electrolysis device blow torch( vid of, my chan) and people were weirdos seeing these in action.
Some these were so mystical I got called Devil, Satanic minion.  Called names, Punched in face at Public demonstration of Electrolysis Blow Torch.. Water being turned into Fire, was, to much for dedicated christian.. I was doing Dark Magic, satanic stuff.
 Another time, my itty bitty Tesla coil scared others that had little science education, but immense religious Indoctrination 😢, got thrown out of holiday party.
 Scary is Ignorant people, worse is just how their ignorance is maintained and guided. Taught to act against, their own best intrests😮😢

author: @dennissorensen8765
text: What seemed most magical to me, was the pressure induced energy generation on those plates with a layer of isolating material between two conductive materials, or what is was. I didn't manage to fully understand it, and I can't find the video again. 🙈

replies.author: @ThinkingandTinkering
replies.text: it's a capacitor acting as an electron pump mate - my friend researches them - she's a genius

replies.author: @dennissorensen8765
replies.text:
@ThinkingandTinkering  Thanks mate! I'm further behind in my understanding of physics than to be able to make sense of those terms, but I have a massive testbed for trying out the potential for making some panels of that kind myself, as you did in the video. That being my metal roof, which I aim to replace a good portion of next spring. So, I thought of making a winter project out of making some panels to mount on the northern side of the roof and see what the rain can do on the side where the sun is not going to do much. 😊

Is it possible that I might persuade you to give me the number of the longest video you made about that? 🙏😊😊

author: @12thsonofisrael
text: Thank you 🕊

replies.author: @ThinkingandTinkering
replies.text: You are so welcome

author: @billschwandt1
text: Very well said. I write about this frequently but rarely say it as beautifully as you just did. Great video.
